notes: Played one scenario, everything worked fine
title: Gloomhaven

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 with Max-Q Design
notes: Tested with Proton 4.2-9, 3.16-9, 3.7-8. Best results with 3.16-9; game lags every 5 mins on the dot. 4.2-9 lags enough to be unplayable, and 3.7-9 crashes. Changing resolution or graphics quality has no effect under any version of Proton.
title: Gloomhaven

notes:
Playing on 'fastest' graphics setting to achieve a reasonable frame rate, haven't compared performance with Windows.

Also works with PROTON_USE_WINED3D11 on my intel laptop.

I haven't noticed any 'lag' every 5 minutes.
title: Gloomhaven

notes: The first time that I launched the game, it launched okay. Subsequent attempts failed, and I was forced to change proton versions to get it to run. However, I changed back to 4.2-9, and it worked again . The game runs choppily, which I think is not intended. Animations are not very smooth, and there's some latency between my actions and the game's responses. It's playable though. I've hit some weird behaviors (like healing failing to heal a character), but I don't know whether those are related to steam play or not.
title: Gloomhaven

notes:
Played several scenarios (.8047 release). Everything seems to work fine. The only tweak I had to make was upgrading the graphics driver from v390.116 to v430.26 in order to get the characters to appear. Without that upgrade I could see their clothes, but not their bodies.
Linux Mint 19.1 only lists driver versions up to 390 in its driver manager. Had to add a PPA from https://launchpad.net/~graphics-drivers/+archive/ubuntu/ppa to get an updated version.
title: Gloomhaven

notes: Perfect game, running flawlessly. Pick it up if you get a chance it's an amazing strategy game/rpg.
title: Gloomhaven

notes: Played through the tutorial. Worked flawlessly.
title: Gloomhaven
